creator: sue@welearn.com.au
text:
On Mon, Jul 26, 1999 at 03:41:29PM -0700, jobaldwi@vt.edu wrote: > + > +

Every UNIX process is completely firewalled off from every > + other UNIX process. One process can modify the address space > + of another. This is unlike Windows where a process can easily > + overwrite the address space of any other, leading to a crash. > + Oops, wasn't this identified as a typo? IIRC a "not" was omitted: One process cannot modify the address space of another. -- Regards, -*Sue*-
